If the match is postponed, the market will remain open until it is completed; if it is canceled with no make-up game, the market resolves to "0-0." The primary resolution source for this market will be the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if final official statistics are not published within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's superior form and Southampton's dismal scoring record—managing just five league goals all season—anchor trader consensus around clean-sheet victories at 0-1, 0-2, or 0-3, each hovering near 50% implied probability. Recent developments amplify this: Southampton's five-match losing streak includes shutouts in three straight home games, exposing their blunt attack amid injuries to Che Adams and Cameron Archer. Arsenal, meanwhile, welcome back Declan Rice from suspension and boast a rested squad post-midweek rest, with 70% win rate in away fixtures against bottom-six sides. The tight clustering reflects uncertainty over Arsenal's exact margin in a low-scoring affair, tempered by historical head-to-heads averaging 2.8 total goals.

question: Will the Patek Index hit $112,000 (HIGH) by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, for any day between market creation and April 30, 2026, the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Patek price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Subdial — specifically, the Patek Index chart available at https://subdial.com/market. The daily values in USD shown on the chart will be used for resolution. 

This market refers to prices displayed in USD. To switch the display currency, use the currency toggle located at the left beneath the graph and select “USD” instead of “GBP”.

This market will resolve as soon as the price shown on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Patek chart reaches the listed price, or once the value for April 30, 2026, is published, and the listed price has not been reached. If no data for April 30, 2026, has been published by May 14, 2026, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to published data points on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Patek chart before the April 30, 2026 data point has been published will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after the April 30, 2026, data point has been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Patek Philippe watch prices reaching the target threshold by April 30 hinges primarily on softening secondary market demand, with the Chrono24 Luxury Watch Index showing Patek models down 12% year-to-date amid high interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending. Recent Sotheby's and Christie's auctions fetched 8-15% below 2023 peaks for key references like the Nautilus 5711, reflecting broader luxury sector weakness tied to China's economic slowdown and reduced collector speculation. Market-implied odds capture this caution, backed by $MM in trader capital, though a rebound could spark if Watches and Wonders (April 23-27) unveils strong new releases boosting hype. Watch U.S. consumer confidence data this week for sentiment shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Patek Philippe watch prices reaching the target threshold by April 30 hinges primarily on softening secondary market demand, with the Chrono24 Luxury Watch Index showing Patek models down 12% year-to-date amid high interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending. Market-implied odds capture this caution, backed by $MM in trader capital, though a rebound could spark if Watches and Wonders (April 23-27) unveils strong new releases boosting hype. Watch U.S. consumer confidence data this week for sentiment shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 35% implied probability that Rolex secondary market prices will hit the specified threshold by April 30, reflecting cooling luxury demand amid persistent high interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending. The WatchCharts Rolex Price Index stands at 92.5 as of late March, down 12% year-to-date and 25% from 2022 peaks, driven by Rolex's ramped-up production flooding gray markets and softening Chinese buyer sentiment amid economic headwinds. Key risks include further U.S. CPI data on April 10 and potential Fed rate cut signals, which could boost wealth effects; a breach below 90 would signal trader consensus for deeper declines, while resilient auction results might stabilize odds.

The resolution source for this market is Subdial — specifically, the Rolex Index chart available at https://subdial.com/market. The daily values in USD shown on the chart will be used for resolution. 

This market refers to prices displayed in USD. To switch the display currency, use the currency toggle located at the left beneath the graph and select “USD” instead of “GBP”.

This market will resolve as soon as the price shown on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Rolex chart reaches the listed price, or once the value for April 30, 2026, is published, and the listed price has not been reached. If no data for April 30, 2026, has been published by May 14, 2026, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to published data points on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Rolex chart before the April 30, 2026 data point has been published will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after the April 30, 2026, data point has been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 35% implied probability that Rolex secondary market prices will hit the specified threshold by April 30, reflecting cooling luxury demand amid persistent high interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending. The WatchCharts Rolex Price Index stands at 92.5 as of late March, down 12% year-to-date and 25% from 2022 peaks, driven by Rolex's ramped-up production flooding gray markets and softening Chinese buyer sentiment amid economic headwinds. Key risks include further U.S. CPI data on April 10 and potential Fed rate cut signals, which could boost wealth effects; a breach below 90 would signal trader consensus for deeper declines, while resilient auction results might stabilize odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts against Audemars Piguet secondary market prices reaching the $100,000 threshold for popular Royal Oak models by April 30, with implied probabilities hovering around 35%, reflecting real capital backing skepticism amid luxury sector headwinds. Softer demand from China—luxury sales down 20% YoY per Bain & Company—coupled with elevated U.S. interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending, has driven average AP prices 12-18% lower YTD on platforms like Chrono24. Supply gluts from authorized dealers add downward pressure, though Watches & Wonders (April 1-7) could spark short-term hype if new releases excite collectors. Traders eye UBS luxury indices for confirmation, acknowledging resolution hinges on verified Chrono24 averages.

The resolution source for this market is Subdial — specifically, the Audemars Piguet Index chart available at https://subdial.com/market. The daily values in USD shown on the chart will be used for resolution. 

This market refers to prices displayed in USD. To switch the display currency, use the currency toggle located at the left beneath the graph and select “USD” instead of “GBP”.

This market will resolve as soon as the price shown on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Audemars Piguet chart reaches the listed price, or once the value for April 30, 2026, is published, and the listed price has not been reached. If no data for April 30, 2026, has been published by May 14, 2026, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to published data points on the Bloomberg x Subdial Watch Index for Audemars Piguet chart before the April 30, 2026 data point has been published will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after the April 30, 2026, data point has been finalized will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts against Audemars Piguet secondary market prices reaching the $100,000 threshold for popular Royal Oak models by April 30, with implied probabilities hovering around 35%, reflecting real capital backing skepticism amid luxury sector headwinds. Softer demand from China—luxury sales down 20% YoY per Bain & Company—coupled with elevated U.S. interest rates curbing high-net-worth spending, has driven average AP prices 12-18% lower YTD on platforms like Chrono24. Supply gluts from authorized dealers add downward pressure, though Watches & Wonders (April 1-7) could spark short-term hype if new releases excite collectors. Traders eye UBS luxury indices for confirmation, acknowledging resolution hinges on verified Chrono24 averages.

question: Israeli forces enter Beirut by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israeli military personnel physically enter the municipality of Beirut in Lebanon for military purposes by the listed day, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, aerial and maritime operations will not be considered. Only operations involving troops on the ground within the municipality of Beirut will count.

Undercover Israeli operatives and assets will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be photo and video evidence, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli ground operations remain confined to southern Lebanon border areas following the IDF's limited incursion launched October 1 against Hezbollah targets, with no confirmed advances toward Beirut, over 100 km north. Recent airstrikes have hit Hezbollah sites in Beirut suburbs, killing senior leaders like Hassan Nasrallah, but ground entry into the capital would mark major escalation amid U.S.-brokered ceasefire efforts and UN Security Council pressure. Trader consensus reflects low odds on near-term entry, weighing Hezbollah's degraded rocket capabilities against Israel's stated aims of border security over full invasion, with diplomatic talks and potential hostage deals as key upcoming catalysts that could de-escalate.

question: Will fewer than 10 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between March 17-23?
description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from March 17, 2026, through March 23, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for March 23, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Strait of Hormuz transits for March 17-23 clusters tightly around 35-45 ships, mirroring recent weekly averages of 38-42 merchant vessels tracked via AIS data amid stable Persian Gulf shipping patterns. No major disruptions from Iranian naval actions, U.S. Fifth Fleet patrols, or Houthi spillover threats have materialized early in the week, sustaining higher probability bins like 40-44 at 27% and 45+ at 22%. The race stays neck-and-neck as partial counts through March 20 align with norms, but remaining days leave room for variance from weather, security alerts, or commercial adjustments. Separation could emerge from real-time incident reports, weekend tanker surges, or official maritime tallies confirming flows.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for March 23, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

events.title: How many ships transit the Strait of Hormuz this week? (Mar 17-23)
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from March 17, 2026, through March 23, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

No major disruptions from Iranian naval actions, U.S. Fifth Fleet patrols, or Houthi spillover threats have materialized early in the week, sustaining higher probability bins like 40-44 at 27% and 45+ at 22%. The race stays neck-and-neck as partial counts through March 20 align with norms, but remaining days leave room for variance from weather, security alerts, or commercial adjustments. Separation could emerge from real-time incident reports, weekend tanker surges, or official maritime tallies confirming flows.

question: Will Bitcoin be the top performing crypto for the week of March 16?
description:
This market will resolve to the listed crypto asset with the highest percentage change during the week of March 9, 2026.

The “Change” value shown for each asset’s weekly candle for the week of March 16, 2026 will be used. A weekly candle is considered finalized once the following week’s candle is published.

The resolution source is Binance, using the spot USDT trading pairs for each listed asset (e.g. BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT), for example:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT

If two or more assets are tied for the highest percentage change, this market will resolve based on whichever comes first alphabetically.

Only Binance spot USDT-pair data will be used. Prices from other exchanges, trading pairs, or data sources will not be considered.

Weekly candles are defined based on Binance’s chart timezone. For reference, in the ET timezone, the weekly candle for the week of March 16, 2026 is titled “2026/03/15 19:00” on Binance.

question: Richarlison: Anytime Goalscorer
description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Tottenham Hotspur FC and Nottingham Forest FC, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 10:15 AM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Richarlison is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Tottenham Hotspur FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Premier League game between Tottenham Hotspur FC and Nottingham Forest FC, scheduled for March 22, 2026 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tottenham's mounting injury crisis dominates trader sentiment for player props in their Boxing Day clash with Nottingham Forest, with James Maddison sidelined by an ankle issue, Micky van de Ven out long-term, and Richarlison absent, potentially limiting Son Heung-min's assist props and boosting cards markets for Spurs' makeshift defense. Forest enter in strong away form, unbeaten in five road games, fueling over bets on Morgan Gibbs-White shots or assists amid their counterattacking style. Recent head-to-heads favor Tottenham at home, but current odds reflect crowd wisdom pricing in Spurs' fatigue from a congested schedule versus Forest's rest edge, heightening volatility for goalscorers like Solanke or Hudson-Odoi.

question: Will CZ post 0-19 posts from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between March 20, 12:00 PM ET and March 27, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between March 20, 12:00 PM ET and March 27, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

question: Will Elon Musk post 240-259 tweets from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 20 12:00 PM ET to March 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

With no confirmed 2026 events like Mars updates or midterms disrupting his rhythm, leading ranges compete tightly on baseline habits versus viral catalysts, as cultural icon status fuels unpredictable spikes in public discourse. Volatility persists, with traders eyeing historical precedents for over/under thresholds.

question: Will Elon Musk post 480-499 tweets from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 20 12:00 PM ET to March 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.
